name: gptk

metadata.name: gptk
metadata.summary: A General Python Tool Kit, suitable for most projects.

documentation:
# GPTK: the General Python Tool Kit
This is a collection of code that I have found useful in my 
tinkerings, perhaps you'll find it useful, too.




## Installation
```sh
pip install gptk
```

## Usage
Use pydoc to peruse the package.  Everything included should be well
documented.

modules.name: gptk
documentation:
J.T. Hartzfeld's Personal Library
This is a collection of generally useful snippets and bits that make
their way into most of my projects.

modules.name: gptk.enums
documentation: Enumerations of various natural and constructed phenomena.

classes.name: gptk.enums.Priority
documentation:
A simple priority enumeration for general classification.

Priorities run from 0 to 7 because 8 bins of stuff-to-do is
plenty.

classes.name: gptk.enums.Planets
documentation: The EIGHT planets in the Solar system.

classes.name: gptk.singletons.Singleton

methods.name: gptk.singletons.Singleton.__new__
documentation: Return the canonical instance of the class.

methods.name: gptk.singletons.Singleton.__init__
documentation:
CAUTION: DANGEROUS OVERRIDE!
This __init__ contains logic to prevent unintended 
    reinitialization. If overridden, this is ought 
    to be considered.

:keyword arguments:
    reinit: bool; if true, run self.init() again;
                    otherwise, do nothing.

methods.name: gptk.singletons.Singleton.init
documentation:
Implement this method as a stand-in for __init__, who is "quite pre-
occupied at the moment, thank you very much."

Classic Singleton implementation as an Abstract Base Class. Simply
subclass Singleton, override init, and implement whatever function-
ality is required.  The __new__ and __init__ details are already
handled.

EXAMPLE:
class Registry(Singleton):
    def init(self, *args, **kwargs):
        self.data = kwargs.get('data', None)
        ...

modules.name: gptk.encoding
documentation: Classes to encode and decode text data in various(currently 1) formats.

classes.name: gptk.encoding.AsInt

methods.name: gptk.encoding.AsInt.encode
documentation: Return an integer which uniquely encodes the given string.

methods.name: gptk.encoding.AsInt.decode
documentation: Return the string encoded (base64/utf-8) by the given integer.

Static methods to store arbitrary python strings as integer values
representing base64 encoded text.
